Title: AtMYB41 activates ectopic suberin synthesis and assembly in multiple plant species and cell types

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: lipid polyester; wax-associated lipids; phenylpropanoid polymer
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protects against water loss and biotic/abiotic stresses; barrier formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root endodermis and periderm normally; ectopic accumulation in leaf epidermal and mesophyll cell walls upon overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes ectopic accumulation of aliphatic suberin monomers, suberin-associated waxes, and elevated lignin/phenylpropanoid deposition; induces suberin-like lamellae in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Sufficient single-gene activator for ectopic suberin synthesis and assembly across species and cell types
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of suberin biosynthesis, associated wax production, and phenylpropanoid/lignin pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GPAT5; CYP86A1; CYP86B1; FAR1; FAR4; FAR5; ASFT; KCS2/DAISY; FACT; PAL1; CCoAMT1; CAD5; C4H/REF3; PRXR9/PER30
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly activates suberin biosynthetic genes and increases phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthetic gene transcript abundance; little or no activation of most cuticle biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; NaCl/salt stress; abiotic stress; phosphorylation by MPK6 reported
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter active in root endodermal and surrounding cortical cells under ABA or NaCl treatment, but not under unstressed conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis, stress-inducible in endodermis/cortex; ectopic expression via 35S in leaves caused metabolic reprogramming; transient 35S expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves caused similar effects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; transient expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling; TEM; qRT-PCR; thioacidolysis lignin analysis; GUS reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB41 overexpression in Arabidopsis leaves caused accumulation of 4.5-fold more suberin-type than cutin-type monomers and induced suberin-like lamellae. 2. Transient AtMYB41 expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves caused 22-fold more suberin-type than cutin-type monomers and induced lamellae in epidermal and mesophyll cells. 3. AtMYB41 strongly upregulated major suberin biosynthetic genes including GPAT5, CYP86A1, CYP86B1, ASFT, FAR1/4/5, KCS2 and FACT, while also increasing phenylpropanoid/lignin gene expression and lignin monomer abundance. 4. The AtMYB41 promoter is activated by ABA and NaCl in root endodermal cells, supporting a role in stress-induced suberization.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana; native AtMYB41 promoter used for GUS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment; 200 mM NaCl treatment; abiotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MPK6 (stress-induced phosphorylation reported from prior work)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not experimentally established in this paper; authors note MYB factors often function with bHLH, WD40, and other MYBs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering enhanced barrier properties and stress-responsive suberization; potential tool for renewable production of bifunctional fatty acids/bioplastics feedstocks, but strong constitutive overexpression causes stunted growth and surface defects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stunted growth, glossy malformed leaf surfaces, elevated toluidine-blue permeability, altered pavement cell shape, ectopic wall deposition
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray re-analysis of published AtMYB41 overexpression lines; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding to target promoters was not shown; knockout lines were unavailable, so necessity was not established. AtMYB45 and AtMYB67 did not phenocopy suberin induction, supporting specificity.
